objectives: Define core concepts and scope of embedded systems and digital systems
Set up an integrated development environment software for embedded development
Establish basic programming workflow and project structure
Apply tools for software configuration management to manage source and changes
learning_path.title: Embedded Systems Foundations and Development Workflow

objectives: Explain key Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M) concepts relevant to firmware design
Connect and validate basic digital I/O using Hardware Interfacing & Digital Electronics
Implement simple embedded C++ (Embedded) modules aligned with hardware behavior
learning_path.title: Arm Cortex-M Microcontroller Architecture and Hardware Interfacing

objectives: Design interrupt-driven control flow for responsive embedded behavior
Configure and use timers for periodic scheduling and measurement
Use DMA to move data efficiently while minimizing CPU load
learning_path.title: Interrupt-Driven Firmware: Interrupts, Timers, and DMA

objectives: Explain memory regions and constraints typical in embedded targets
Apply Memory Management & Linker Scripts to control placement and footprint
Diagnose common memory-related issues in embedded builds
learning_path.title: Memory Layout, Memory Management, and Linker Scripts

objectives: Select an appropriate serial bus based on system constraints and requirements
Implement and validate peripheral communication using standard interfaces
Integrate bus communication into an embedded application structure
learning_path.title: Device Communication: SPI, I2C, UART, and CAN

objectives: Structure an application into concurrent tasks with clear responsibilities
Apply RTOS timing and event mechanisms to meet real-time needs
Integrate interrupt and driver interactions within an RTOS-based design
learning_path.title: Real-Time Scheduling with FreeRTOS/Zephyr

objectives: Explain Embedded Linux architecture and how it differs from bare-metal and RTOS approaches
Set up a basic Embedded Linux development workflow in an integrated development environment software
Integrate application components with system services and interfaces
learning_path.title: Embedded Linux Fundamentals and System Integration

objectives: Map peripheral communication needs to appropriate driver interfaces
Implement and validate peripheral access paths for SPI/I2C/UART/CAN in an Embedded Linux context
Troubleshoot driver-level integration issues using a structured workflow
learning_path.title: Embedded Linux Device Drivers and Peripheral Access

objectives: Apply automatic programming to generate or transform embedded code artifacts
Integrate automatically produced outputs into an existing embedded project structure
Validate correctness and maintainability of automatically produced code in the build workflow
learning_path.title: Automatic Programming for Embedded Development Acceleration

objectives: Use logic programming to express system rules, constraints, or expected behaviors
Apply logic programming outputs to support embedded systems design decisions
Create a repeatable workflow that connects logic programming artifacts to programming tasks
learning_path.title: Logic Programming for Specification and Verification in Embedded Contexts

matched_occupation.description: Embedded systems software developers program, implement, document and maintain software to be run on an embedded system.
matched_occupation.label: embedded systems software developer
